speaker: CONSTABLE
text: Ô diable!

speaker: ORLÉANS
text: Ô Seigneur! Le jour est perdu, tout est perdu!

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Mort de ma vie, all is confounded, all!

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Reproach and everlasting shame

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Sits mocking in our plumes.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Ô méchante Fortune!

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Do not run away.

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: Why, all our ranks are broke.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: O perdurable shame! Let’s stab ourselves.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Be these the wretches that we played at dice for?

speaker: ORLÉANS
text: Is this the king we sent to for his ransom?

speaker: BOURBON
text: Shame, and eternal shame, nothing but shame!

speaker: BOURBON
text: Let us die. In once more! Back again!

speaker: BOURBON
text: And he that will not follow Bourbon now,

speaker: BOURBON
text: Let him go hence, and with his cap in hand

speaker: BOURBON
text: Like a base pander hold the chamber door,

speaker: BOURBON
text: Whilst by a slave, no gentler than my dog,

speaker: BOURBON
text: His fairest daughter is contaminate.

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: Disorder, that hath spoiled us, friend us now.

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: Let us on heaps go offer up our lives.

speaker: ORLÉANS
text: We are enough yet living in the field

speaker: ORLÉANS
text: To smother up the English in our throngs,

speaker: ORLÉANS
text: If any order might be thought upon.

speaker: BOURBON
text: The devil take order now! I’ll to the throng.

speaker: BOURBON
text: Let life be short, else shame will be too long.
